# Source Card: Stable Long-Term Risk of Leukaemia in Patients with Severe Congenital Neutropenia Maintained on G-CSF Therapy

## Summary
According to PubMed, this peer-reviewed prospective SCNIR registry study of 374 severe congenital neutropenia (SCN) patients on long-term G-CSF establishes the diagnostic ANC threshold for SCN (persistent ANC < 0.5 ×10^9/L) and quantifies the long-term competing risks of MDS/AML versus sepsis death. It revises the earlier unstable estimate downward: the annual MDS/AML hazard reaches a stable plateau of 2.3%/year after 10 years on G-CSF (not the ~8%/year previously feared). For a pediatric CDS module these are cohort-level surveillance/risk-stratification anchors, not diagnostic or treatment directives. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-2141.2010.08216.x

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) SCN diagnosed by ANC persistently < 0.5 ×10^9/L with marrow maturation arrest — locator: Introduction para 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f790a646797ddda8bfc421f99227529abd702e4a2aba4a3eefc6576b8efbf16b]"
- (ev_002) MDS/AML hazard plateaus at 2.3%/year after 10 years on G-CSF — locator: Results para 2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8923f94b4c43d9f8768efea66a09252f9d096d4200e3bd49d2de30067a8c7520]"
- (ev_003) Sepsis-death hazard stable at 0.81%/year — locator: Results para 2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:4c86e8c22c497a0bda509a15e16f5e1c8f1a1a5982801fecbae8ed89e4485ced]"
- (ev_004) 15-year cumulative incidence 10% sepsis death / 22% MDS/AML — locator: Results para 3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:eb62211768414da75b41c3dc73c7815a6af988da11abb06b974820411356b0c8]"
- (ev_005) Risk-stratification medians: ANC 2.188 ×10^9/L and G-CSF dose 8 μg/kg/day — locator: Results para 5 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:72d25848ddd3b905664120ea4cd532717d7ef74268cb302fc2d8e7c8e33237d6]"
- (ev_006) MDS/AML hazard rises 1.24-fold per doubling of G-CSF dose — locator: Results para 4 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:5dcc9aa2aecde93ce285c87e1349cc6857e17726ed06bd88a9f513b6113d9e98]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: severe congenital neutropenia — a congenital disorder generally diagnosed in infancy/childhood; the SCNIR cohort (n=374) is not age-stratified in this report, so pediatric-only subrates are NOT locatable here.
- Assay/method: ANC values derive from CBC with differential; the source does not specify analyzer or method, so the diagnostic and median thresholds are stated method-independently. Hazard/cumulative-incidence figures are statistical model outputs (spline hazards, NPMLE cumulative incidence, Cox models), not lab assays.
- Lifecycle: 2010 report; explicitly supersedes the 2001 SCNIR MDS/AML hazard estimate (~8%/year, range 4–12%/year). No formal guideline version.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The high-risk subgroup (below-median ANC 2.188 ×10^9/L despite at/above-median G-CSF 8 μg/kg/day) is flagged by the authors as warranting evaluation for early haematopoietic stem-cell transplantation and close leukaemia-transformation monitoring. This is a source-noted escalation signal, NOT an executable transplant/treatment directive — it must surface to the CDS converter as a referral/surveillance proposal only.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Superscript loss: PMC full-text extraction dropped scientific-notation exponents (rendered "0.5 ×10/l" and "2.188×10/l"). Standard SCN convention and units confirm these are ×10^9/L; flagged so ingest does not mis-parse the magnitude. The verbatim quotes above preserve the extracted (superscript-stripped) text exactly.
- No pediatric-only stratification: age-band-specific MDS/AML or sepsis rates are NOT locatable in this source — recorded as a GAP, not inferred.
- Conflict preserved (not averaged): this report's 2.3%/year 10-year MDS/AML plateau directly revises the prior 2001 SCNIR estimate of ~8%/year (stated range 4–12%/year). Both figures are retained; the 2010 value supersedes but the conflict is named, not silently collapsed.
- Cohort-level hazards are population risk anchors, not individual decision rules; no diagnosis/treatment/dosing directive is asserted.
